fn id(a: A): produce A / {} := produce a
---
id : (A) -> produce A / {}
==========
fn keep(a: A): produce A / {} := produce a
---
keep : (A) -> produce A / {}
==========
fn f(a: A): produce A / {} := let x = a in produce x
---
f : (A) -> produce A / {}
==========
fn bad(): produce A / {} := produce x
---
ERROR: unknown variable `x`
==========
fn bad(a: A): produce B / {} := produce a
---
ERROR: type mismatch: expected B, got A
==========
fn pick(a: A): produce A / {} := match a { x => produce x }
---
pick : (A) -> produce A / {}
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn pick2(a: OptionA, b: A): produce A / {} := match a { .some(let x) => produce x, .none => produce b }
---
pick2 : (OptionA, A) -> produce A / {}
==========
data Option { .some(A), .none }
fn bad(a: Option): produce Option / {} := match a { .some(let x, let y) => produce a, .none => produce a }
---
ERROR: pattern `some` expects 1 args, got 2
==========
data Option { .some(A), .none }
fn bad2(a: Option): produce Option / {} := match a { .nope => produce a }
---
ERROR: unknown variant `nope` in match pattern
==========
data OptionInt { .some(B), .none }
fn ok(a: OptionInt, b: B): produce B / {} := match a { .some(x) => produce x, .none => produce b }
---
ok : (OptionInt, B) -> produce B / {}
==========
data OptionInt { .some(B), .none }
fn bad3(a: OptionInt, c: C): produce B / {} := match a { .some(x) => produce c, .none => produce c }
---
ERROR: type mismatch: expected B, got C
==========
data Pair { .pair(B, C) }
data Wrap { .wrap(Pair), .empty }
fn nested_ok(w: Wrap, b: B): produce B / {} := match w { .wrap(.pair(x, _)) => produce x, .empty => produce b }
---
nested_ok : (Wrap, B) -> produce B / {}
==========
data Pair { .pair(B, C) }
data Wrap { .wrap(Pair), .empty }
fn nested_bad(w: Wrap, b: B): produce B / {} := match w { .wrap(.nope(x)) => produce b, .empty => produce b }
---
ERROR: unknown variant `nope` in match pattern
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn ne(b: Bool): produce Bool / {} := match b { .true => produce b }
---
ERROR: non-exhaustive match: missing patterns .false
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn ex(b: Bool): produce Bool / {} := match b { .true => produce b, _ => produce b }
---
ex : (Bool) -> produce Bool / {}
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn unreach1(b: Bool): produce Bool / {} := match b { _ => produce b, .true => produce b }
---
ERROR: unreachable match arm: pattern already covered
==========
data Bool { .true, .false }
fn unreach2(b: Bool): produce Bool / {} := match b { .true => produce b, .true => produce b, .false => produce b }
---
ERROR: unreachable match arm: pattern already covered
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn mk(a: A): produce OptionA / {} := OptionA.some(a)
---
mk : (A) -> produce OptionA / {}
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn badmk(a: A): produce OptionA / {} := produce OptionA.some(a)
---
ERROR: expected value expression
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn badp1(a: OptionA, b: A): produce A / {} := match a { some(x) => produce b, .none => produce b }
---
ERROR: constructor pattern must start with `.`
==========
data OptionA { .some(A), .none }
fn badp2(a: OptionA, b: A): produce A / {} := match a { some => produce b, .none => produce b }
---
ERROR: variant pattern `some` must be written `.some`
